qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated admin-equivalent access enables server-side script execution in the default configuration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same advisory root cause: `server/settings.default.js` ships with `secureEnabled` commented out, and `verifyGroups()` grants admin-equivalent access when security is disabled. The finding then demonstrates an unauthorized admin-only API path reachable from that exact default-auth-disabled state.

selected_finding.title: Unauthenticated admin-equivalent access enables server-side script execution in the default configuration

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory's exact vulnerable code and root cause: the shipped default configuration leaves authentication disabled, which causes admin-only API authorization to collapse for unauthenticated requests. A fresh judge-side execution of the copied PoC against FUXA v1.2.7 then produced unauthorized server-side command execution, which is stronger than the advisory's stated unauthorized API access and project modification impact and is caused by that same default-auth-disabled condition.
evidence: The advisory explicitly identifies `server/settings.default.js` in FUXA v1.2.7, where `secureEnabled` is commented out by default and the application starts with authentication disabled.

In the reproduced v1.2.7 source, `server/api/index.js` `verifyGroups()` returns `authJwt.adminGroups[0]` whenever `runtime.settings.secureEnabled` is false, giving unauthenticated requests admin-equivalent authorization for protected APIs.
In the reproduced v1.2.7 source, `POST /api/projectData` stores attacker-controlled script objects and `POST /api/runscript` executes them through the server-side script subsystem.

The fresh PoC run under `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_796bda46/.judge/poc_runs/unauthenticated_admin_script_rce/run.log` returned a live `uid=501(lumi) gid=20(staff) ...` string from an unauthenticated script execution request, proving the advisory-matched auth-disabled state enabled unauthorized protected action and server-side code execution.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC against cloned FUXA v1.2.7 created a server-side script through the unauthenticated admin-only project mutation API and then executed it through `/api/runscript`, returning host command output beginning with `uid=` in the HTTP response.
